qa_pairs.question: How can users directly employ Intel Math Kernel Library (MKL) when compiling with Intel compilers such as `ifort`, `icc`, or `icpc`?
qa_pairs.answer: To directly use Intel MKL with Intel compilers, users should replace `<tt>-lblas</tt>` and `<tt>-llapack</tt>` in their compiler and linker options with either `<tt>-mkl=sequential</tt>` or `<tt>-mkl</tt>`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the distinction between using the `<tt>-mkl=sequential</tt>` and `<tt>-mkl</tt>` options when directly linking Intel Math Kernel Library (MKL) with Intel compilers?
qa_pairs.answer: When directly linking Intel MKL with Intel compilers, the `<tt>-mkl=sequential</tt>` option will not utilize internal threading, whereas the `<tt>-mkl</tt>` option will enable internal threading.
